person_name: Marcus Octavius, Tribune of the Plebs (2nd c. BCE, opponent of Tiberius Gracchus)
person_name_full: Marcus Octavius, Tribune of the Plebs (2nd c. BCE, opponent of Tiberius Gracchus)
notes: Tribune of the Plebs in 2nd century BCE, known as an opponent of Tiberius Gracchus and described as steady and orderly.

docs.title: Life of Tiberius Gracchus
docs.author: Plutarch
docs.excerpt: … who, being a young man of a steady, orderly character, and an intimate friend of Tiberius, upon this account declined at first the task of opposing him; but at length, over-persuaded with the repeated importunities of numerous considerable persons, he was prevailed upon to do so, and
